content.problemStatement:
Let's call an integer sequence nice if the sum of its elements is divisible by a given integer KKK. You are given an integer sequence A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã. Hasan wanted to find the number of nice subsequences of this sequence. However, this problem is too easy, so Hasan decided to add an extra constraint.

For an arbitrary non-empty sequence of indices i1<i2<‚Ä¶<iLi_1 \lt i_2 \lt \ldots \lt i_Li1‚Äã<i2‚Äã<‚Ä¶<iL‚Äã, let's call a subsequence Ai1,Ai2,‚Ä¶,AiLA_{i_1}, A_{i_2}, \ldots, A_{i_L}Ai1‚Äã‚Äã,Ai2‚Äã‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AiL‚Äã‚Äã very nice if it is nice and iL‚àíi1‚â•Wi_L-i_1 \ge WiL‚Äã‚àíi1‚Äã‚â•W. Help Hasan and find the number of very nice subsequences. Since this number may be very large, compute it modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
constraints: 0‚â§Ai‚â§K‚àí10 \le A_i \le K-10‚â§Ai‚Äã‚â§K‚àí1 for each valid iii
the sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚ãÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, KKK and WWW.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚Äî the number of very nice subsequences modulo 109+710^9+7109+7.
